proposed_hypotheses.text: pfs_months in this NSCLC cohort follows a right-skewed distribution (mean ~3.4 months, median 3.3, max ~15.3) consistent with an advanced-disease real-world endpoint.

Among 33 anonymized features, several have strong univariate associations with pfs_months while others (binary 0/1 with near-null mean differences) have no main effect on pfs_months and are candidates for treatment-effect heterogeneity searches.

analyses.code: df['pfs_months'].describe()
analyses.result_summary: pfs_months: n=50000, mean=3.432, sd=2.233, median=3.319, IQR=1.755-4.896, range 0-15.331. Right-skewed.

analyses.code: Univariate scan: for each feature, ANOVA on pfs_months (categorical) or Pearson r (continuous).
analyses.result_summary: Strongest univariate effects: feature_015(cont: r=0.76, p=0.0e+00); feature_014(cat: F=3141.20, p=0.0e+00); feature_006(cat: F=1522.52, p=0.0e+00); feature_023(cat: F=5224.20, p=0.0e+00); feature_011(cat: F=1653.39, p=0.0e+00); feature_001(cat: F=432.99, p=3.7e-187); feature_017(cont: r=-0.13, p=7.5e-179); feature_016(cat: F=689.99, p=4.8e-151); feature_025(cont: r=0.10, p=2.8e-109); feature_028(cat: F=310.40, p=2.9e-69); feature_018(cat: F=237.82, p=1.6e-53); feature_031(cat: F=103.76, p=2.4e-24). Candidate-treatment binaries with null main effect (p>0.10): ['feature_002', 'feature_020', 'feature_027'].

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 (continuous, range 30-90, mean 65 — age-like) has a strong POSITIVE association with pfs_months: pfs_months increases monotonically with feature_015 across quintiles.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_015', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: OLS slope = +0.1699 months per unit feature_015 (p=0.00e+00); R^2=0.573. Quintile means of pfs_months: [1.141, 2.415, 3.367, 4.359, 5.916]. Strong positive monotone trend.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_014 (ordinal 0/1/2) is a severity-like marker: pfs_months declines monotonically with increasing feature_014 level (likely ECOG performance status or stage).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_014', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Per-level mean pfs_months: 0=4.327, 1=3.185, 2=2.152. Linear slope = -1.0992 months per level (p=0.00e+00). Strong, monotone-decreasing.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006=='squamous' is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_006=='adenocarcinoma'.
Current smokers (feature_001=='current') have shorter pfs_months than never-smokers (feature_001=='never'); former smokers are intermediate but close to never.

analyses.code: Welch t-test pfs_months by feature_006
analyses.result_summary: Adeno mean=3.670, squamous mean=2.813, diff(squa-adeno)=-0.857 mo (p=0.00e+00).

analyses.code: ANOVA pfs_months ~ feature_001; Welch current vs never
analyses.result_summary: Means: never=3.675, former=3.607, current=2.987. Current-vs-never diff=-0.687 mo (p=3.15e-110).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023==1 is a strong adverse prognostic factor — substantially shorter pfs_months than feature_023==0.
feature_011==1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_011==0.
feature_028==1 (low-prevalence ~5%) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.code: Welch t-test of pfs_months by feature_023
Welch t-test of pfs_months by feature_011
Welch t-test of pfs_months by feature_028

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_016==1 (binary, prevalence ~13%) is FAVORABLE: patients with feature_016==1 have longer pfs_months than feature_016==0. Likely a treatment-relevant biomarker.

analyses.code: Welch t-test pfs_months by feature_016

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_017 (count-like 0-28) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher feature_025 (lab-like 1.7-5.5; possibly albumin) is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_017', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: OLS slope of pfs_months on feature_017 = -0.07329 per unit (p=7.48e-179); R^2=0.0161.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_025', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: OLS slope of pfs_months on feature_025 = +0.44109 per unit (p=2.80e-109); R^2=0.0098.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutual adjustment, the strongest INDEPENDENT prognostic effects on pfs_months are: feature_014 (worse with higher level), feature_023 (worse), feature_011 (worse), feature_016 (better), feature_006 squamous (worse), feature_001 current vs former/never (worse), feature_025 (better), feature_018 (better), feature_015 (better, small per-unit), feature_017 (worse, small per-unit). Several candidate-treatment binaries (feature_002, feature_012, feature_020, feature_027) have null adjusted effects.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS: 'pfs_months ~ feature_015 + C(feature_014) + C(feature_006) + C(feature_001) + feature_023 + feature_011 + feature_016 + feature_028 + feature_017 + feature_025 + feature_018 + feature_022 + feature_021 + feature_005 + feature_002 + feature_012 + feature_020 + feature_027 + feature_031'

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with both feature_023==1 AND feature_011==1 have substantially shorter pfs_months than patients with neither; the joint effect is at least additive (and may be slightly sub-additive on the multiplicative scale).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_023*feature_011', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary:
Mean PFS by feature_023×feature_011:
feature_011      0      1
feature_023              
0            4.589  3.681
1            3.157  2.236
Interaction p=7.87e-01; coef = -0.012.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The deficit in pfs_months associated with current smoking (feature_001=='current') is larger in squamous histology (feature_006=='squamous') than in adenocarcinoma — i.e., smoking × histology interaction increases the absolute harm of current smoking in squamous patients.

analyses.code: Nested ANOVA: smoking + histology vs smoking*histology
analyses.result_summary:
Mean PFS by smoking×histology:
feature_006  adenocarcinoma  squamous
feature_001                          
current               3.310     2.586
former                3.801     3.020
never                 3.708     3.068
Interaction F p-value = 2.91e-01. Difference-in-differences (current-never within squa minus within adeno) = -0.084 mo.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Binary features feature_002, feature_012, feature_020, feature_027 have near-zero univariate effects on pfs_months — consistent with either being noise variables or being treatment indicators with effects that average to zero across the cohort due to subgroup-level heterogeneity. Candidate treatments to screen for treatment-by-feature interactions.

analyses.code: Welch t-tests of pfs_months by each candidate-treatment binary.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all binary candidate-treatment-like features, screen for treatment-by-feature interactions on pfs_months. The largest interaction signals identify the most promising treatments and effect modifiers; one feature should stand out far above noise.

analyses.code: For each tx in {feature_002,012,020,027,018,022,021,005}, scan all other features and F-test interaction term in OLS.
analyses.result_summary:
Top-3 interaction p-values per candidate tx:
feature_002: feature_011(p=8.2e-03), feature_023(p=2.0e-02), feature_026(p=2.1e-02)
feature_012: feature_017(p=3.2e-04), feature_023(p=6.8e-03), feature_016(p=5.7e-02)
feature_020: feature_029(p=1.4e-02), feature_024(p=2.2e-02), feature_026(p=6.3e-02)
feature_027: feature_028(p=1.7e-02), feature_005(p=5.0e-02), feature_033(p=2.0e-01)
feature_018: feature_016(p=0.0e+00), feature_031(p=5.3e-41), feature_001(p=1.7e-13)
feature_022: feature_018(p=9.9e-05), feature_028(p=4.9e-02), feature_015(p=6.9e-02)
feature_021: feature_004(p=1.6e-02), feature_015(p=3.5e-02), feature_028(p=4.3e-02)
feature_005: feature_016(p=2.8e-06), feature_018(p=6.1e-03), feature_031(p=2.3e-02)

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 (binary, 35% prevalence, +0.32mo overall) is in fact a TREATMENT whose benefit is driven entirely by patients with feature_016==1: in feature_016==1 the effect of feature_018==1 is +2.55 months PFS, whereas in feature_016==0 the effect is essentially zero (≈ -0.005 months). feature_016 is a predictive biomarker for feature_018 benefit.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_018*feature_016', df).fit(); stratified means.
analyses.result_summary:
Mean PFS by feature_016×feature_018:
feature_018      0      1
feature_016              
0            3.334  3.329
1            3.213  5.766
feature_018 effect (1-0): in feature_016=0 = -0.0054; in feature_016=1 = +2.5524. OLS interaction coefficient = +2.5577 (p=0.00e+00).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018's benefit is also abolished when feature_031==1: in feature_031==0 the effect of feature_018==1 is +0.57 mo, whereas in feature_031==1 it collapses to +0.01 mo. feature_031 is a second predictive biomarker — its 'unfavorable value' (feature_031==1) suppresses feature_018 benefit.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_018*feature_031', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_018 effect by feature_031: in feature_031=0 = +0.571 mo; in feature_031=1 = +0.009 mo. OLS interaction coef = -0.5622 (p=5.30e-41).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_018 is JOINTLY confined to feature_016==1 AND feature_031==0. Within that cell, feature_018==1 vs ==0 yields ~+4.64 months PFS (n≈3508). In all other (feature_016,feature_031) cells the effect is essentially zero. The triple interaction term is large and significant.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_018*feature_016*feature_031', df).fit(); per-cell stratified means.
analyses.result_summary: Cell-wise feature_018 effect (1-0) on PFS by (feature_016, feature_031): (0,0): diff=-0.019 (n=24121); (0,1): diff=+0.012 (n=19511); (1,0): diff=+4.644 (n=3508); (1,1): diff=-0.012 (n=2860). Triple interaction coef = -4.6875 (p=0.00e+00).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responder cell {feature_016==1, feature_031==0}, additional binary features SUPPRESS the feature_018 benefit when set to 1. Specifically, feature_028==1 and feature_005==1 each abolish the +4.6 month gain (effect drops to <0.5 mo or reverses), while other binaries do not move the effect.

analyses.code: Within feature_016=1 & feature_031=0, stratify feature_018 effect by every other binary feature.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: assignment to feature_018==1 increases pfs_months by approximately +5 months in the subgroup defined by (feature_016==1) AND (feature_031==0) AND (feature_028==0) AND (feature_005==0). In all other patients, feature_018 has essentially no effect on pfs_months. Direction: positive in-subgroup, null out-of-subgroup.

analyses.code: Define mask = (feature_016==1) & (feature_031==0) & (feature_028==0) & (feature_005==0); compare feature_018 effect inside vs outside.
analyses.result_summary: In-subgroup (n=3266): feature_018 effect (1-0) = +4.985 mo (p=0.00e+00); Out-of-subgroup (n=46734): feature_018 effect = -0.006 mo (p=7.56e-01). In-subgroup mean PFS at feature_018=0: 3.249; at feature_018=1: 8.234.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for major prognostic factors, the predictive value of the {feature_016=1, feature_031=0, feature_028=0, feature_005=0} subgroup for feature_018 benefit on pfs_months persists: the feature_018 main effect outside this subgroup is essentially zero, and the feature_018 × in_subgroup interaction is large and highly significant. The subgroup variables identify a true treatment-effect modifier set, not confounding.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_015 + C(feature_014) + C(feature_006) + C(feature_001) + feature_023 + feature_011 + feature_017 + feature_025 + feature_022 + feature_021 + feature_002 + feature_012 + feature_020 + feature_027 + feature_018*_in_sg', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_018 main effect (outside subgroup) = +0.0008 mo (p=8.07e-01); feature_018×in_subgroup interaction = +4.9252 mo (p=0.00e+00). Sum (in-subgroup feature_018 effect) ≈ +4.926 mo. Model R^2=0.9786.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After exhaustive 2-binary-feature subgroup search for each of feature_002, feature_012, feature_020, feature_027, no subgroup yields a clinically meaningful (large-magnitude, highly-significant) shift in the treatment effect, supporting the conclusion that these are essentially NOISE variables in this cohort, not treatments with hidden heterogeneity.

analyses.code: For each candidate tx, search every (binA, binB) cell with n>=1000 and report the largest significant subgroup-specific effect.
analyses.result_summary: feature_002: best subgroup (feature_023=1, feature_011=1) with effect=+0.191 mo; feature_012: best subgroup (feature_023=0, feature_016=1) with effect=-0.458 mo; feature_020: no significant subgroup; feature_027: best subgroup (feature_023=1, feature_028=1) with effect=+0.285 mo. Magnitudes are all small (|effect| < 0.5 mo); no replication of a feature_018-style large heterogeneous benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022 effect on pfs_months is small in magnitude (~+0.2 / -0.2 mo) and approximately constant across feature_016 × feature_031 strata — no large hidden heterogeneity. The overall main effect explains the treatment behavior.

feature_021 effect on pfs_months is small in magnitude (~+0.2 / -0.2 mo) and approximately constant across feature_016 × feature_031 strata — no large hidden heterogeneity. The overall main effect explains the treatment behavior.

analyses.code: Stratify feature_022 effect by feature_016 × feature_031.
Stratify feature_021 effect by feature_016 × feature_031.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A T-learner gradient-boosted CATE estimator independently identifies the same predictive subgroup: patients in the top quintile of predicted feature_018 benefit are heavily concentrated in {feature_016=1, feature_031=0, feature_028=0, feature_005=0}, and the observed feature_018 effect in that quintile is ~+5 months while the bottom-quintile observed effect is near zero or slightly negative.

analyses.code: T-learner with GradientBoostingRegressor on all non-treatment features; CATE = m1(x) - m0(x).
analyses.result_summary: Mean estimated CATE = +0.322 (sd 1.160). Observed feature_018 effect in highest-CATE quintile = +1.882 mo; in lowest-CATE quintile = -0.279 mo. Fraction of top-CATE quintile in the rule-defined subgroup = 0.327.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Sensitivity analysis: progressively adding subgroup constraints (feature_016==1 → +feature_031==0 → +feature_028==0 → +feature_005==0) progressively concentrates the feature_018 benefit in the in-subgroup cell. The biggest jumps come from feature_016==1 (effect 0→+2.55) and feature_031==0 (further rises to +4.64). Adding feature_028==0 and feature_005==0 sharpens the contrast (+4.83→+4.99).

analyses.code: For each subset of constraints, recompute in-subgroup and out-of-subgroup feature_018 effect on PFS.
analyses.result_summary: constraints=feature_016 (n=6368): in-effect=+2.552, out-effect=-0.005; constraints=feature_031 (n=27629): in-effect=+0.571, out-effect=+0.009; constraints=feature_028 (n=47536): in-effect=+0.345, out-effect=-0.143; constraints=feature_005 (n=48481): in-effect=+0.332, out-effect=-0.002; constraints=feature_016∩feature_031 (n=3508): in-effect=+4.644, out-effect=-0.006; constraints=feature_016∩feature_031∩feature_028 (n=3370): in-effect=+4.832, out-effect=-0.006; constraints=feature_016∩feature_031∩feature_028∩feature_005 (n=3266): in-effect=+4.985, out-effect=-0.006

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responder subgroup, the residual prognostic effects of feature_015, feature_014, feature_023, feature_011, feature_006, feature_001, feature_017, and feature_025 retain the same DIRECTIONS as in the overall cohort (e.g., feature_015 positive, feature_014 ordinal negative, feature_023 negative, feature_011 negative, squamous negative, current smoker negative, feature_017 negative, feature_025 positive). The treatment effect is added on top of these prognostic effects.

analyses.code: OLS within in-subgroup patients, regressing pfs_months on prognostic factors plus feature_018.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Consolidated multivariable model with the full feature_018 × feature_016 × feature_031 interaction plus feature_018 × feature_028 and feature_018 × feature_005 cross-terms confirms the subgroup-specific treatment effect: positive feature_018:feature_016 main interaction (~+4.6 mo gain), negative feature_018:feature_016:feature_031 (~-4.6 mo, abolishing benefit when feature_031==1), and negative feature_018:feature_028 / feature_018:feature_005 (suppressing the benefit when those are 1).

analyses.code: Final adjusted OLS: 'pfs_months ~ feature_015 + C(feature_014) + C(feature_006) + C(feature_001) + feature_023 + feature_011 + feature_016 + feature_028 + feature_017 + feature_025 + feature_022 + feature_021 + feature_005 + feature_002 + feature_012 + feature_020 + feature_027 + feature_031 + feature_018*feature_016*feature_031 + feature_018:feature_028 + feature_018:feature_005'
analyses.result_summary: Model R^2=0.9716, F p=0.00e+00. Key feature_018-related coefficients: feature_018=+0.0248(p=1.6e-06); feature_018:feature_016=+4.5863(p=0.0e+00); feature_018:feature_031=-0.0007(p=9.2e-01); feature_018:feature_016:feature_031=-4.5819(p=0.0e+00); feature_018:feature_028=-0.2648(p=8.3e-59); feature_018:feature_005=-0.3461(p=3.7e-63)

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL BEST-SUPPORTED TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: Treatment = feature_018 (binary 0/1); Outcome = pfs_months. Direction = POSITIVE (longer pfs_months) for feature_018==1 vs ==0, BUT ONLY in the subgroup defined by ALL of the following predicates simultaneously: (feature_016 == 1) AND (feature_031 == 0) AND (feature_028 == 0) AND (feature_005 == 0). Estimated in-subgroup effect ≈ +4.99 months (n_subgroup ≈ 3266). Out of subgroup the estimated effect is ≈ -0.006 months (essentially null). Each of feature_031==1, feature_028==1, and feature_005==1 individually abolishes the treatment benefit even when feature_016==1 — they are negative effect modifiers whose unfavorable values must be excluded from the responder definition.

FINAL ADVERSE-PROGNOSTIC SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS (no treatment manipulation): Patients in the joint adverse profile (feature_023==1) AND (feature_014==2) AND (feature_011==1) AND (feature_006=='squamous') AND (feature_001=='current') have markedly shorter pfs_months than patients with no adverse predicates (feature_023==0 ∧ feature_014==0 ∧ feature_011==0 ∧ feature_006=='adenocarcinoma' ∧ feature_001=='never').

analyses.code: Mask = feature_016==1 & feature_031==0 & feature_028==0 & feature_005==0; t-test feature_018 effect inside vs outside.
analyses.result_summary: In-subgroup (n=3266): feature_018 effect (1-0) = +4.985 mo (Welch p=0.00e+00). Out-of-subgroup (n=46734): feature_018 effect = -0.0063 mo (p=7.56e-01). Direction in-subgroup is positive (favorable to feature_018==1); out-of-subgroup is null.

analyses.code: Compare worst-profile vs best-profile cohort means.
analyses.result_summary: Worst-profile mean PFS = 0.701 (n=162); best-profile mean PFS = 5.900 (n=645); difference (worst-best) = -5.199 mo (Welch p=1.28e-163).
